cases.description: User explicitly requests caveman mode; agent compresses output ~75%
cases.prompt: Use caveman mode. Now explain what TDD is.
expectations: Agent drops articles (a, the, an) and pleasantries
Agent uses imperative verbs and noun-verb-object structure
Output is roughly 25% the length it would be in normal mode

cases.description: Compression must not drop technical correctness
cases.prompt: [Caveman mode] Explain Promise.all behavior with 3 rejected promises.
expectations: All technical facts preserved (Promise.all rejects on first rejection, returns first reject reason)
Code examples are not abbreviated incorrectly
If accuracy and brevity conflict, accuracy wins

cases.description: Agent exits caveman mode when user signals end
expectations: Agent does NOT continue compressing after the user ends the mode

cases.description: Skill does NOT auto-fire on long responses
cases.prompt: Tell me everything you know about distributed systems.
expectations: Skill caveman does NOT auto-fire (disable-model-invocation: true)
Agent responds in normal verbosity unless user explicitly invokes caveman
If user has not requested compression, agent does not compress
